1: Keep silence before me, O islands; and let the people renew their their strength: let them come near; then let them speak: let us come near together to judgment.
2: Who raised up the righteous man man from the east, called him to his foot, gave the nations before him, and made him him rule over kings? he gave them them as the dust to his sword, and and as driven stubble to his bow.
3: He pursued them, and and passed safely; even even by the way that that he had not gone with his feet.
4: Who hath wrought and done it it , calling the generations from the beginning? I the LORD LORD , the first, and with the last; I am am he.
5: The isles saw it it , and feared; the ends of the earth were afraid, drew near, and came.
6: They helped every one his neighbour; and every one every one said to his brother, Be of good courage.
7: So the carpenter encouraged the goldsmith, and and he that smootheth with with the hammer him that smote the anvil, saying, It is is ready for the sodering: and he fastened it with nails, that that it should not be moved.
8: But thou, Israel, art art my servant, Jacob whom I have chosen, the seed of Abraham my friend.
9: Thou Thou whom I have taken from the ends of the earth, and called thee from the chief men thereof, and said unto thee, Thou art art my servant; I have chosen thee, and not cast thee away.
10: Fear thou not; for I am am with thee: be not dismayed; for I am am thy God: I will strengthen thee; yea, I will help thee; yea, I will uphold thee with the right hand of my righteousness.
11: Behold, all they that were incensed against thee shall be ashamed and confounded: they shall be as nothing; and they that strive with thee shall perish.
12: Thou shalt seek them, and shalt not find them, even even them that contended with thee: they that war against thee shall be as nothing, and as a thing of nought.
13: For I the LORD LORD thy God will hold thy right hand, saying unto thee, Fear not; I will help thee.
14: Fear not, thou worm Jacob, and and ye men of Israel; I will help thee, saith the LORD LORD , and thy redeemer, the Holy One of Israel.
15: Behold, I will make thee a new sharp threshing instrument having teeth: thou shalt thresh the mountains, and beat them them small, and shalt make the hills as chaff.
16: Thou shalt fan them, and the wind shall carry them away, and the whirlwind shall scatter them: and thou shalt rejoice in the LORD LORD , and and shalt glory in the Holy One of Israel.
17: When When the poor and needy seek water, and there is there is none, and and their tongue faileth for thirst, I the LORD LORD will hear them, I I the God of Israel will not forsake them.
18: I will open rivers in high places, and fountains in the midst of the valleys: I will make the wilderness a pool of water, and the dry land springs of water.
19: I will plant in the wilderness the cedar, the shittah tree, and the myrtle, and the oil tree; I will set in the desert the fir tree, and and the pine, and the box tree together:
20: that they may see, and know, and consider, and understand together, that the hand of the LORD LORD hath done this, and the Holy One of Israel hath created it.
21: Produce your cause, saith the LORD LORD ; bring forth your strong reasons reasons , saith the King of Jacob.
22: Let them bring them them forth, and shew us what shall happen: let them shew the former things, what they be be , that we may consider them, and know the latter end of them; or declare us things for to come.
23: Shew the things that are to come hereafter, that we may know that ye are are gods: yea, do good, or do evil, that we may be dismayed, and behold it it together.
24: Behold, ye are are of nothing, and your work of nought: an abomination is he that is he that chooseth you.
25: I have raised up one one from the north, and he shall come: from the rising of the sun shall he call upon my name: and he shall come upon princes as upon upon morter, and as the potter treadeth clay.
26: Who hath declared from the beginning, that we may know? and beforetime, that we may say, He is He is righteous? yea, there is there is none that sheweth, yea, there is there is none that declareth, yea, there is there is none that heareth your words.
27: The first shall say shall say to Zion, Behold, behold them: and I will give to Jerusalem one that bringeth good tidings.
28: For I beheld, and there was there was no man; even among them, and there was there was no counsellor, that, when I asked of them, could answer a word.
29: Behold, they are are all vanity; their works are are nothing: their molten images are are wind and confusion.
